chats.text: Alright, I'm pulling up the Q3 attainment model for our discussion. And you know what? We've historically speaking not had this much runway before.
Did you hear that about Ryan's trajectory-defying week? I heard it was 'high leverage'. That's a code word for 'someone else's problem'.
I'm going to take a two-minute call. If I don't come back, assume we're in attainment shortfall territory.
Team time prevalence has been trending down since Q2. We need to address the velocity gap proactively before we're in executional drag territory.
Noted. Will loop in the backup squad for coverage.
{NAME} — Hey, saw you were offline during that Ryan window too. Did your team actually get hit or was that just a nice story?
Action item: all leads to verify no downstream work is stranded.
If we can't backfill on a trajectory-defying week, does that make us... non-consequential? Right?
can we wrap up? i have a foursome at 6:45. my golf handicap is literally tied to Q3 attainment so— right?
historically speaking, when someone goes offline during high leverage windows the velocity drops 40%. that's not my call to make right?
the downstream risk here isn't Ryan's output—it's our team time prevalence during his absence. how do we mitigate that? right?
i think what we need is some heightened synergy between the silence. like, can we get people to actually read before replying? right?
anyone have any other thoughts before i sign off? right?
if I may— given Ryan's unavailability we're creating an executional drag, which is fine, right? the mitigation plan is... let me check.
so the question becomes: can someone own the throughput gap? because i have a tee time at 6:30 sharp. right?
i'm going to wrap up here. if you need anything else feel free to message me after 7pm but my golf handicap is non-negotiable right?
